title: BSD as Descent Problem — p-adic BSD via Berkovich

analysis.object: T858: BSD as Descent Problem — p-adic BSD via Berkovich depth analysis

padic_bsd.description: p-adic BSD (Mazur-Tate-Teitelbaum): L_p(E,1) controls E(Q_p) and Sha[p^inf]

padic_l_function_berkovich.description: p-adic L-function L_p(E,s) is a p-adic analytic function -- lives in Berkovich space

berkovich_descent_padic.description: Berkovich descent (T775, T777): applies to any algebraic variety including modular curves

descent_for_padic_bsd.description: p-adic BSD -> tropical p-adic BSD -> Berkovich descent -> classical BSD

mtt_conjecture.description: MTT: L_p(E,1)/L(E,1) = (1 - a_p^{-1})^2 * (p-adic unit). Berkovich descent handles this ratio.

bsd_is_descent.description: BSD is a descent problem: p-adic L-function (Berkovich EML-3) descends to classical L-function (algebraic EML-2)
bsd_is_descent.reason: BSD = descent from EML-3 to EML-2

t858_theorem.description: T858: BSD is a descent problem. p-adic L-function (Berkovich EML-3) -> classical L-value (EML-2) via formal GAGA. Berkovich descent machinery (T775) applies. T858.

analysis.theorem: T858: BSD as Descent Problem — p-adic BSD via Berkovich (S1138).

key_theorem: T858: BSD as Descent Problem — p-adic BSD via Berkovich (S1138).
